original: Heraclius' fleet takes Constantinople
4th Council of Constantinople (8th ecumenical council) opens
King Alfonso VII of Leon recognises Portugal as a Kingdom
Paris theologian Jean de Fayt warns against the Flagellants at Avignon
Jews are expelled from Lower Bavaria by order of Ludwig IX
Eedgenootschap signs anti-French Saint League
Foundation of the city of Concepción, Chile
Spanish army under Alva's son Don Frederik plunders Mechelen
Henry Casimir I appointed viceroy of Groningen
The University of Kiel is founded.
Haarlem people's revolution due to food shortages
Opera "Orfeo Ed Euridice" by Christoph Willibald Gluck is first produced in Vienna in front of Empress Maria Theresa.
Spain declares war on England
King Willem I mobilizes Dutch army
Mormon pioneer Captain Lot Smith and members of the Utah militia destroy US army supply wagon train in Wyoming during Utah War
The City of Anaheim is founded
Battle of Hatchie Bridge, Tennessee; confederate forces avoid capture by Union army
Federal fleet occupies Galveston, Texas
Confederate sub David damages Union ship Ironsides
Battle of Allatoona: Confederate troops under Major General Samuel G. French fail to dislodge a Union garrison in Bartow County, Georgia
Most of Calcutta is destroyed by a cyclone, approximately 60,000 die
Last day of Julian calendar in Alaska

A strong hurricane known as the Saxby Gale devastates the Bay of Fundy region in Maritime Canada. Incredibly, British naval officer Stephen Martin Saxby predicts the storm 10 months earlier in December 1868 through astronomy.
Man-made tunnel under St. Anthony Falls, Minnesota collapses, threatening the Minneapolis riverfront
Palace Hotel on Market Street opens in San Francisco
The Dalton Gang ends in shoot-out in Coffeyville, Kansas bank holdup; 4 members killed, and a fifth captured
First UK individual cycling time trial is held on a 50-mile course north of London; Frederick Thomas Bidlake organiser
William Gillette's dramatic play "Secret Service" premieres in NYC
Canadian Interprovincial Rugby Football Union (IRFU) plays first game; Montreal beats Toronto, 17-8 in Montreal
Bulgaria declares independence from Turkey, Ferdinand I becomes Tsar
Chicago White Sox pitcherEd Walshbeats Detroit Tigers, 6-1, his 40th victory of the MLB season; forces AL pennant race to the final day
Portugal overthrows monarchy, proclaims republic
Italian troops occupy Tripoli
Boston Red Sox beat Philadelphia Athletics, 3 - 0 for their 105th win of the MLB season, an AL record until 1927 NY Yankees (110)
NY Highlanders last game at Hilltop stadium; beat Washington, 8-6; Brooklyn Dodgers last game at Washington Park; lose 1-0 v NY Giants
World War I first aerial combat resulting in a kill
Allies land troops at the northern Greek city of Salonika; Greece is nominally neutral but allows the landing
Bulgaria enters World War I as one of the Central Powers.
Norwegian population agrees to prohibition
Present constitution of Liechtenstein comes into effect
NY Yankees and NY Giants play out a controversial 3-3 tie in 10 innings in Game 2 of Baseball World Series at Polo Grounds, Manhattan, NYC; Giants win series, 4-0-1
The Lacarno Conference meets: Germany agrees to recognise their frontiers with France and Belgium and will apply for membership in the League of Nations
WSM-AM in Nashville Tenn begins radio transmissions
British airship crashes in storm at Beauvais, France, 48 die
Homecoming of the bodies of Swedish polar balloon expedition (1897) led bySalomon August Andrée
1st nonstop transpacific flight, Japan to Wash (Herndon & Pangborn)
Paul Green's play "House of Connelly" premieres in NYC
With new ownership Detroit Falcons officially change name to Detroit Red Wings, and the Winged Wheel is introduced; previous names Cougars (1926-30), Falcons (1930-32)
The Jarrow March sets off for London
Dutch Minister Carl Romme says unemployment is 25% "quarter of Romme"
5,000 Jews of Dubno, Russia massacred
US air raid on Wake, Japanese execute 98 US prisoners in retaliation
Harold Arlen and Yip Harburg's musical "Bloomer Girl" premieres in NYC
Kerkrade is liberated in the Netherlands
Suffrage is extended to women in France
"Meet the Press" premieres on radio
Hollywood Black Friday: A six month strike by Hollywood set decorators turns into a bloody riot at the gates of Warner Brothers' studios
Indonesian army forms
1st Cannes Film Festival ends

Newcastle United equals English Football League record for biggest winning margin in a 13-0 thrashing of Newport in a Division 2 match at St. James' Park; Len Shackleton scores 6, Charlie Wayman 4 for the Toon
WBNS TV channel 10 in Columbus, OH (CBS) begins broadcasting
WOR (now WWOR) TV channel 9 in NY-Secaucus, NY (IND) begins
19th congress of Communist Party meets in Moscow
The first documented recovery meeting of Narcotics Anonymous is held.
Hurricane Hazel forms in the Caribbean, killing 400-1000 people in the Bahamas and Haiti
Yugoslav dissident Milovan Djilos sentenced to 7 years
KRTV TV channel 3 in Great Falls, MontanaT (CBS) begins broadcasting
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
British Labour party demands unilateral nuclear disarmament
Eastern AL Electra turbo-prop crashed in Boston Harbor (61 die)
KEYC TV channel 12 in Mankato, MN (CBS) begins broadcasting
China & Nepal sign treaty
The Beatles release their first record, "Love Me Do"
Hyde St Pier re-opens as State Historical Park
KIXE TV channel 9 in Redding, CA (PBS) begins broadcasting
San Francisco Fire Department Museum dedicated
Chuck Linster performs 6,006 consecutive push-ups
Dick McInnes from Henderson, Kentucky stays aloft almost 12 hours in a kite pulled by a speedboat along the Ohio River
Civil rights march in Derry stopped by the Royal Ulster Constabulary (RUC)and resulting clashes lead to two days of serious rioting, often considered the start of 'the Troubles' in Northern Ireland
KPLM (now KESQ) TV channel 42 in Palm Springs, CA (ABC) begins
American fiddle player "Papa" John Creach joins Jefferson Airplane
PBS becomes a US television network.
Quebec separatists kidnap British trade commissioner James Cross
A new sitting of the Northern Ireland parliament at Stormont begins, though the Social Democratic and Labour Party remain absent due to its continuing protest against Internment
Herbert Mullin 1st kills, claiming it was to prevent earthquakes
Signature of the European Patent Convention.
Guildford pub bombing by the IRA leaves 5 dead and 65 injured.
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
MLB expansion draft: Seattle Mariners & Toronto Blue Jays pick 30 unprotected AL players; Ruppert Jones OF (Seattle) & Bob Bailor OF (Toronto) are first picks
Isaac Bashevis Singer wins the Nobel Prize for literature
Over 30 major nations ratify the Environmental Modification Convention, which prohibits weather warfare that has widespread, long-lasting or severe effects
Sweden's Fälldin government falls
Formal opening of meditation hall of Zenshin Temple in Riverdale, the Bronx NYC
Dutch guilder/Deutsche mark revalued up 5.5%
Swedish diplomat Raoul Wallenberg becomes an honorary American (posthumously)
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
First regular season NHL game is played in New Jersey (Newark; NJ Devils play a 3-3 tie against Pittsburgh Penguins at Brendan Byrne Arena; Don Lever scores first Devils' goal
Hernan Siles Zuazo appointed president of Bolivia
Unmanned rocket sled reaches 9,851 kph at White Sands, NM
Panamanian jockey Jorge Velasquez wins his 5,000th career race, riding Banquet Scene to victory in the 4th race at Belmont Park
Grambling's Eddie Robinson wins record 324th football game
London Sunday Times reports Israel is stocking nuclear arms
Brazil adopts its Constitution
Israel bans Meir Kahane's Kach Party on grounds of racism
"Henry and June" film, based on the book by Anaïs Nin, first film to be given an NC-17 rating in the US, released
Cincinnati jury acquits art gallery of obsentity (Mappelthorpe photos)
Pittsburgh Penguins left wing Kevin Stevens has 2 goals & 4 assists in 7-4 opening-night win against the Washington Capitals at Capital Centre to set NHL record for most points in a season opener (6)
Fresno State ties NCAA football record with 49 points in a quarter (2nd), as they route New Mexico, 94-17 at Bulldog Stadium, Fresno
Military transport plane crashes at Jakarta, 133 dies
The first official version of the Linux kernel, version 0.02, is released.
USSR reduces nuclear weapons arsenal
NY senator Alphonse D'Amato filibusters for 15 hours 20 mins
US Air goes on strike
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
52 members of the cult Order of the Solar Temple (Zonnetempel) die in a mass murder-suicide in Salvan, Switzerland
Cleveland Indians strike out 23 Baltimore Orioles in 12 innings in 4-3 ALDS win at Jacobs Field; Orioles win series, 3-1
New York centre fielder Bernie Williams homers from each side of the plate as the Yankees beat Texas, 6-4 to win the AL Division Series, 3 - 1
STS 86 (Atlantis 20) lands
The Ladbroke Grove rail crash in west London kills 31 people.
TV show "Gilmore Girls" created by Amy Sherman-Palladino debuts on The WB, starring Alexis Bledel, Lauren Graham, Kelly Bishop, and Edward Herrmann

MLB's Atlanta Braves become first pro sports team to win 10 straight division titles after mauling the Marlins, 20-3 to clinch the NL East title; NBA's Boston Celtics (1957-65) & LA Lakers (1982-90) both won 9 in a row
Robert Stevens becomes the first victim in the 2001 anthrax attacks.
For the first time in its 42-year old history, the Anaheim Angels win a post-season series by beating the Yankees, 9-5 to take the ALDS, 3-1
Chicago Cubs win their first MLB postseason series since the 1908 World Series with 5-1 win v Atlanta Braves in Game 5 NLDS at Turner Field
Kansas City wide receiver Dante Hall scores on game winning 93-yard punt return in Chiefs 24-23 victory v Denver Broncos at Arrowhead Stadium; returns for score in an NFL-record 4th straight game
NHL greatWayne Gretzkymakes his coaching debut for the Phoenix Coyotes in 3-2 loss to the Vancouver Canucks at Rogers Arena

NHL opens regular season after 10-month lockout of entire 2004-05 season; Ottawa's Daniel Alfredsson & Dany Heatley first players to score winning goals in a shoot out in NHL history; both score v Toronto goalie Ed Belfour in 3-2 OT victory
African Burial Ground in Manhattan is the first National Monument, dedicated to the first Africans of early New York and Americans of African descent
Australia wins cricket's Champions Trophy at Centurion Park in Gauteng, South Africa by 6 wickets (28 balls remaining) v New Zealand; chasing 200, Shane Watson is 105 not out in Australia's 206/4
Injured Indian cricket batsman VVS Laxman is the hero with 73 not out as the home team chases down 216 to beat Australia with just 1 wicket to spare in the 1st Test at Mohali
Anglo Platinum Limited fires 12,000 striking workers in South Africa
New Zealand beats South Africa, 38-27 in Johannesburg to retain the Rugby Championship with an undefeated record; All Black flanker Liam Messam crosses for 2 tries
Floods in South Carolina a "1,000-year storm" result in 12 deaths and 9 dams to fail
Nobel prize for Medicine awarded to William C. Campbell, Satoshi Ōmura (Roundworm) and Youyou Tu (Malaria)
Trans-Pacific Partnership agreement announced by trade ministers of 12 countries in Atlanta
Nobel Prize in Chemistry jointly awarded to Jean-Pierre Sauvage, Fraser Stoddart and Bernard Feringa "for the design and synthesis of molecular machines"
Iraqi forces claim victory and control over Islamic State's last urban stronghold Hawija, Northern Iraq
Banksy work "Girl With Balloon" automatically shreds moments after being sold for 1 million pounds in London, renamed "Love is in the Bin"
Nobel Peace Prize awarded jointly to Congolese gynaecologist Denis Mukwege and Yazidi activist Nadia Murad for "efforts to end the use of sexual violence as a weapon of war"
Record price at auction for a living female artist as Jenny Saville's "Propped" sells for £9.5 million in London
US unemployment figures hit lowest level since 1969 - 3.7% according to Department of Labor
At least 14m tonnes of plastic pieces are at the bottom of the ocean, 30 times more than on the surface according to new research
India COVID-19 death toll passes 100,000, with 6.6 million known cases, 3rd highest death toll in the world behind the US and Brazil
Nobel Prize for Medicine awarded jointly to Harvey J. Alter, Michael Houghton and Charles M. Rice for the discovery of hepatitis C virus
WHO estimates 10% of world's population may have been infected with COVID-19, more than 20 times the number of confirmed cases
480 Otis wins Alaska's Fat Bear Week for pre-hibernation weight gain for the third year in a row
Major French investigation finds clergy sexually abused more than 200,000 children over 70 years, most of the victims boys
Nobel Prize for Physics awarded to Syukuro Manabe, Klaus Hasselmann and Giorgio Parisi for pioneering work that warned of climate change
Russia launches an actor and director to the International Space Station to make the first film in orbit
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Carolyn Bertozzi, Morten Meldal and K. Barry Sharpless (his 2nd) for their work on Click Chemistry
Worcester Warriors players have their contracts terminated as the club enters administration
Nobel Prize for Literature awarded to Norwegian author Jon Fosse
Record 961 migrating birds die after hitting McCormick Place Lakeside Center building in Chicago during fall migration
Russian missile attack on a cake holding wake in Ukrainian village of Hroza kills at least 51 people
Pope Stephen IV crownsLouis the PiousHoly Roman Emperor at Reims cathedral
DukePhilip the Goodof Burgundy visits Brussels
French KingLouis XIsigns peace withCharles the Stout
Conference of York begins: trial againstMary, Queen of Scots
William I of Orange's army occupies Brabant
Carlo Goldoni's "Il Teatro Comico" premieres in Venice
French Revolution: Christianity is disestablished in France.
Battle of the Thames; American forces under GeneralWilliam Henry HarrisondefeatTecumseh's Confederacy and their British allies led by Henry Procter near Chatham, Upper Canada
Carl Maria von WebervisitsLudwig van Beethoven
Chief Josephand his people surrender to the US Army, ending theNez PerceWar in the Western United States
Swedish explorerSalomon August Andrée's ill-fated balloon expedition to the North Pole reaches White Island
Samuel Griffith is appointed the first Chief Justice of Australia and SirEdmund Bartonand Richard O'Connor are appointed as foundation justices
Orville andWilbur Wright's make a circling flight on "Flyer III" of 24.2 miles (38.9 kms) in 39 minutes and 23 seconds
Detroit Tigers speedsterTy Cobbsteals his 96th base of the season in 5-0 loss to Cleveland Indians; stands as MLB record until 1962 (Maury Wills, 104)
Edwin Hubbleidentifies Cepheid variable star
Budy Massacre atAuschwitzsub-camp, 90 French-Jewish women beaten to death by prison guards
Queen Wilhelminavisits Rotterdam
Harry Trumandelivers the first presidential address televised from the White House
Sportswriter Robert Ruark first reports golfing greatBobby Jones's rare spinal disease (syringomyelia) which prevent him from playing any form of golf again
Boston Celtics owner Walter Brown & coachRed Auerbachdraw lots out of hat for 3 members of defunct Chicago Stags franchise; hit jackpot with future 6-time NBA champion,Bob Cousy
Jackie Gleasondebuts the character of bus-driver Ralph Kramden on 1st "The Honeymooners" skit (Cavalcade of Stars)
Earl Warrensworn in as 14th Chief Justice of US Supreme Court
10th General Conference on Weights and Measures opens, will define the Kelvin as the primary unit of temperature (afterWilliam Thomson, Lord Kelvin)

Police vice squad officers raid dressing rooms during concert intermission at Music Hall in Houston, Texas and arrest musicians Illinois Jacquet and Dizzy Gillespie for playing dice games, and singerElla Fitzgeraldand her assistant for allowing it; foursome are whisked to the police station, pay their fines, and return to venue to play second set
"Breakfast at Tiffany's" film, based on the novella byTruman Capote, directed byBlake Edwards, and starringAudrey Hepburn, George Peppard, and Buddy Ebsen, premieres
"Dr. No," the first James Bond film based on the novel by Ian Fleming and starringSean Conneryand Ursula Andress, premieres in London
Little Richardjoins Everly Brothers Tour
Nigerian government presents a copy ofBen Enwonwu's sculpture "The Awakening" to the United Nations
TV sketch comedy series "Monty Python's Flying Circus" premieres on BBC1
"Goodbye Yellow Brick Road" 7th studio album byElton Johnis released
AustrianNiki Laudawins the United States Grand Prix at Watkins Glen to claim his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship; wins by 19.5 points fromEmerson Fittipaldiof Brazil
Australian Williams driver Alan Jones wins the United States East Grand Prix to claim his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship; wins by 13 points fromNelson Piquetof Brazil
Edmonton super starWayne Gretzkyscores a goal and assist in 5-4 win over Toronto in the Oilers season opener; starts NHL record 51-game scoring streak; ends 28/1/84
Polish solidarity leaderLech Wałęsawins the Nobel Peace Prize
13th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41G): Challenger 6 launches withMarc Garneau, the first Canadian in space, on board
Rams running backEric Dickersonruns for an NFL overtime record 42-yard touchdown as LA defeats Tampa Bay Buccaneers, 26-20 at Anaheim Stadium
Chile votes in a referendum 56-44 against extendingAugusto Pinochet's regime by 8 years, thus ending the dictator's 16½ years in power
Columbia Records releases "Interiors", the seventh studio album by American singer-songwriterRosanne Cash. produced by Cash herself
US congress votes to overrideGeorge H. W. Bush's veto of a bill regulating cable TV companies, 1st overturn of a Bush veto
Russian PresidentBoris Yeltsinremoves honor guard fromLenin's mausoleum

NBA shortens the 3-point distance to a uniform 22 feet in attempt to help offensive players score more;Michael Jordansets career highs in 3-point attempts & converted 3-point field goals, nearly double previous statistics
Yao Wenyuanis released from prison after serving 20 years for his role in the Chinese Cultural Revolution
Mass demonstrations in Belgrade culminate in the resignation of Serbian strongmanSlobodan Milošević, often called the Bulldozer Revolution
Barry Bondshits his 71st and 72nd home runs in 11-10 loss v LA Dodgers at Pacific Bell Park; breaks Mark McGwire's MLB single season HR record
Tom Ridge resigns as Governor of Pennsylvania to become PresidentGeorge W. Bush's Homeland Security Advisor
7-time All-Star and 6-time NBA Champion forwardScottie Pippenannounces his retirement from the NBA and the Chicago Bulls
Vampire novel "Twilight" byStephenie Meyeris first published by Little Brown

After years of angry denials, triple gold medal winning American sprinterMarion Jonesadmits she used steroids 2000-01; pleads guilty to lying to federal investigators; announces retirement in tearful apology; medals returned to USOC
Film "Michael Clayton", a legal thriller starringGeorge Clooney, Tom Wilkinson, andTilda Swinton, is released
UkrainianWladimir Klitschkobeats Russian Alexander Povetkin in a 12 round unanimous points decision in Moscow to retain his WBA, WBO, IBF, IBO heavyweight titles
French F1 driver Jules Bianchi collides with recovery vehicle during Japanese Grand Prix, dies 10 months later; first F1 death due to racing accident in 21 years (Ayrton Senna1994)
Russell Croweowned South Sydney Rabbitohs beat Canterbury Bulldogs, 30-6 to break 43 year drought and win National Rugby League (Australia) Premiership
Governor of CaliforniaJerry Brownsigns a bill giving terminally ill patients the "right to die"
Spanish constitutional court suspends Catalan parliament to prevent declaration of independence
The New York Times publishes an investigation into allegations of sexual harassment against Hollywood producerHarvey Weinstein
Philippine PresidentRodrigo Dutertereveals he has a neuromuscular disease, myasthenia gravis, causing him health problems
US PresidentDonald Trumpleaves Walter Reed National Military Medical Center while still infectious with COVID-19 and returns to the White House
Duhan van der Merwereturns to Edinburgh on a long-term deal after Worcester Warriors contract termination
Around 1,000 soldiers of the Mongol army land on the Japanese island of Tsushima in the first attack ofKublai Khan's Mongol invasion of Japan

Women of Paris march to Versailles in the March on Versailles to confrontLouis XVIabout his refusal to promulgate the decrees on the abolition of feudalism, demand bread, and have the king and his court moved to Paris
Adolf Hitleris wounded in the left thigh by an exploding shell during the Battle of the Somme
